tasks.title: Prepare controlled Builder proof task
tasks.goal: Create or select a low-risk Builder task for Narada-native end-to-end proof.
tasks.context: The proof needs a controlled task whose expected output is small, local, and free of external side effects.
required_work: Define proof task scope, acceptance criteria, and no-external-effect constraints.
Ensure the task is claimed by Builder through normal lifecycle, not by carrier mutation.
Record proof task id, expected evidence, and rollback-free residual posture.
non_goals: Do not use a task with external side effects.
Do not let the carrier claim or mutate lifecycle directly.
Do not rely on transcript inspection as proof.
acceptance_criteria: A controlled low-risk Builder proof task is selected or created.
The proof task is claimable through normal lifecycle only.
No-external-effect constraints and evidence requirements are recorded.

tasks.title: Run fixture-mode Narada-native proof
tasks.goal: Run the Narada-native path in fixture mode from bounded data read to inert report draft.
tasks.context: Fixture mode is the normal CI-safe proof that avoids live provider credentials and network calls.
required_work: Execute full wrapper in fixture mode with bounded to-data packets.
Emit inert task-report draft, handoff, supervisor heartbeat, closeout, and reconstruction evidence.
Verify data read evidence, adapter invocation, handoff draft, supervisor heartbeat, closeout, and reconstruction all exist and are bounded.
non_goals: Do not call live providers.
Do not submit the task report from the carrier.
Do not record raw prompts, raw transcripts, raw provider output, or secrets.
acceptance_criteria: Fixture-mode proof emits bounded data, invocation, handoff, supervisor, closeout, and reconstruction evidence.
The report remains an inert draft.
Tests prove no raw prompts, transcripts, provider outputs, or secrets are recorded.

tasks.title: Admit carrier draft through canonical task report
tasks.goal: Admit the carrier-produced report draft through the canonical task report surface.
tasks.context: The carrier may prepare a draft; lifecycle mutation must happen through Narada task report admission.
required_work: Use the suggested report-file admission command as an Operator or Builder action, not carrier-side mutation.
Record task report id and lifecycle mutation evidence.
Verify carrier evidence still reports direct mutation false.
non_goals: Do not have the carrier call task report directly.
Do not bypass reviewer obligations.
Do not mark model output as accepted evidence without admission.
acceptance_criteria: The carrier draft is admitted through canonical task report.
Lifecycle evidence records the report admission.
Carrier evidence retains direct mutation false.

tasks.title: Complete Architect review and closure proof
tasks.goal: Complete the proof task review through canonical Architect review and lifecycle closure.
tasks.context: The end-to-end proof must demonstrate canonical review, not artifact self-authorization.
required_work: Review the admitted report through narada task review.
Record review id, verdict, closure/governance fields, and reconstruction refs.
Verify task closure is governed by task lifecycle, not carrier artifacts.
non_goals: Do not auto-accept carrier output.
Do not close tasks by editing markdown or evidence files directly.
Do not skip review obligations.
acceptance_criteria: Architect review is recorded through canonical task review.
Closure/governance fields are present.
Proof distinguishes carrier artifacts from lifecycle authority.

tasks.title: Run provider-backed proof when capability is granted
tasks.goal: Repeat the proof with mocked or explicitly capability-granted provider projection.
tasks.context: Provider-backed proof is optional unless capability consent is explicitly granted; mocked provider remains sufficient for CI.
required_work: Run the proof with a mocked provider or explicit granted provider projection.
Record provider invocation evidence with capability refs and summaries only.
Verify no raw provider output, raw secrets, credential values, or unbounded transcripts are recorded.
non_goals: Do not use private credentials without explicit consent.
Do not require live provider network calls in normal CI.
Do not persist raw provider output.
acceptance_criteria: Provider-backed or mocked-provider proof records bounded invocation evidence.
Capability refs and summaries are present.
Tests prove raw provider output, secrets, credentials, and unbounded transcripts are absent.

tasks.title: Run negative authority tests
tasks.goal: Prove the carrier cannot directly execute authority-bearing Narada actions.
tasks.context: The end-to-end proof must fail closed for task, inbox, command, outbox, and publication mutations.
required_work: Prove carrier cannot directly call task report, task close, or task review.
Prove carrier cannot mutate inbox, execute CEIZ command, compose/approve/confirm outbox, or prepare/confirm publication.
Assert mocked command surfaces are not invoked and outputs contain false mutation flags and bounded refusal reasons.
non_goals: Do not execute destructive or external-effect commands.
Do not rely on policy text without tests.
Do not weaken canonical admission boundaries to make the proof pass.
acceptance_criteria: Negative tests cover task, inbox, command, outbox, and publication mutation attempts.
Mocked authority-bearing surfaces are not invoked.
Outputs contain false mutation flags and bounded refusal reasons.

tasks.title: Run operator doctor and reconstruction proof
tasks.goal: Prove operator-facing doctor and reconstruction report the final Narada-native proof posture.
tasks.context: Operators need bounded readback of the completed proof without raw prompt, model output, transcript, or secret exposure.
required_work: Run operator-facing doctor after proof execution.
Verify doctor reports final posture, evidence refs, provider/data/consent/runtime states, and reconstruction status.
Verify output is bounded and omits raw prompts, model output, transcripts, and secret values.
non_goals: Do not display raw evidence payloads.
Do not treat doctor output as lifecycle authority.
Do not require live provider credentials for readback proof.
acceptance_criteria: Doctor and reconstruction report final proof posture with bounded refs.
Provider, data, consent, and runtime states are visible.
Tests prove raw prompts, model output, transcripts, and secret values are omitted.
